topic_title: Vectors — a list of numbers representing multiple properties at once
verdict: PASS. All 6 checks pass. Corpus is fit to ground downstream artifacts.

soft_findings.text: A word represented in a large AI language model might use a 768-dimensional vector — 768 separate numbers, each capturing a different nuance of that word's meaning.
soft_findings.note: Specific dimension figure (768) lacks an inline citation marker. Surrounding sentences in the paragraph are cited [1][3]. Does not drop below the 60% threshold. Soft note only.

length_sanity.note: ~2700 words is solidly within the standard profile range.

topic_scope.headline: Vectors — a list of numbers representing multiple properties at once
topic_scope.note: All references to downstream concepts (dot product, matrix, embeddings, feature engineering) are contained to single name-and-defer sentences with explicit 'covered later' pointers. The 768-dimensional LLM vector example in §5.3 is used to illustrate dimension (an in-scope concept) and does not teach embedding mechanics. No concept is explained/defined at depth beyond the headline scope.

beginner_readability.note: All non-everyday terms (vector, scalar, dimension, feature, feature vector, feature engineering) are defined on first use or explicitly referenced to a prior topic. No paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms simultaneously. Register is conversational throughout; average sentence length well under 25 words; no hedging phrases detected.
